question: Given that HistoryMessage now supports an optional images field and toOpenAIMessages builds a multimodal content array with images first then text, what would happen today if you tried to run `aura -m qwen3-vl:4b --image ./screenshot.png "describe this"` — walk through exactly where in the pipeline it breaks.

Design the minimal CLI flag needed to wire a --image <path> argument into Aura so it reads a file, base64-encodes it, and attaches it to the HistoryMessage.images field before the first turn. Name the exact function or file that most likely needs to change.

If a user attaches an image to a provider/model that doesn't actually support vision (e.g. a text-only DeepSeek model), what should Aura do — silently drop the image, throw a hard error, or warn and continue text-only? Justify the choice.
Once vision is wired end-to-end, describe one realistic coding task where attaching a screenshot would measurably outperform a text-only description of the same UI bug.

Ruby Alternator is currently forced read-only and text-only. If Ruby is later given qwen3-vl as its model, should Ruby's read-only restriction be loosened before or after it's allowed to receive images? Explain the risk ordering.
